title: Verify image hash
action: Compute the SHA-256 of the acquired image and compare to the value recorded at acquisition. Re-verify before any analysis touches the evidence.
rationale: Establishes evidence integrity and seeds the chain-of-custody record. Any later tamper claim turns on this baseline matching.
expected_result: Computed SHA-256 matches the acquisition log entry and the value on the evidence intake form.
references: SWGDE Best Practices for Computer Forensic Acquisitions §4.4

axiom.action: In AXIOM Process, Tools → Verify Evidence → select SHA-256. Record the computed hash in the case Activity Log and confirm it matches the acquisition entry. AXIOM also re-verifies on processing and surfaces mismatches in the Evidence Sources panel.
axiom.ui_path: AXIOM Process > Tools > Verify Evidence > SHA-256
axiom.notes: Set the hash algorithm in Project Settings if MD5 is also required.

xways.action: In X-Ways, right-click the evidence object → Compute Hash → SHA-256. The result is logged automatically to the case Activity Log. Compare against the acquisition log entry.
xways.notes: X-Ways stores the computed hash on the evidence object so subsequent reports show it without re-computing.

ftk.action: In FTK Imager, File → Verify Drive/Image. Confirm the SHA-256 result matches the acquisition log.
